financial_performance.label: Very Positive
financial_performance.text: LTIMindtree reported revenues of USD 1.18 billion for Q2 FY2026, reflecting a sequential growth of 2.4% in constant currency terms and 2.3% in USD terms. The EBIT margin expanded by 160 basis points from 14.3% in Q1 to 15.9%, driven by an 80 basis points gain from the margin improvement program and a forex tailwind. Profit after tax stood at Rs.1,381 Crores, a 10.1% quarter-on-quarter growth. EPS reached an all-time high of Rs.47.2. The operating cash flow to PAT ratio improved to 85.6%, and the free cash flow to PAT ratio was 72.4%.

forward_guidance.label: Very Positive
forward_guidance.text: Management expressed confidence in maintaining a 'good profitable growth trajectory' and expanding EBIT margins going forward, although specific EBIT guidance was not provided. The order inflow stood at USD 1.59 billion, up 22% year-on-year, with large deals signed across all five verticals. The company remains optimistic about delivering growth in the second half, aiming for 'near double-digit' growth. Seasonal headwinds like furloughs are expected, but management remains confident in margin expansion.

risk_language.label: Positive
risk_language.text: Management acknowledged some pressure within top customers but emphasized this as a 'transitionary phase' with recalibration at the time of renewals. Seasonal headwinds such as furloughs were mentioned, but no substantial risks were expected to delay margin expansion. The commentary on risks was balanced, with management addressing potential challenges while maintaining a positive outlook on growth and margin improvement.

investor_confidence.label: Very Positive
investor_confidence.text: Management projected clarity and confidence, emphasizing strong deal wins and a robust pipeline. Analyst questions were addressed directly, with management providing detailed explanations on margin levers and growth strategies. The call reflected management's credibility, with a focus on strategic transformation and AI investments. The tone was optimistic, with management expressing confidence in sustaining growth momentum.

key_positive_drivers: EBIT margin expansion by 160 basis points
Large deals signed across all five verticals
Strong deal wins and robust pipeline
key_negative_drivers: Recalibration at the time of renewals
notable_phrases: confident of being able to expand our margin further
order inflow stood at USD 1.59 billion
chain_of_thought: The financial performance was strong with significant EBIT margin expansion and record EPS, indicating robust profitability. Forward guidance was optimistic, with management confident in maintaining growth momentum and expanding margins, despite not providing specific EBIT guidance. Risk language acknowledged some customer pressures and seasonal headwinds but was balanced with a positive outlook on overcoming these challenges. Investor confidence was bolstered by management's clarity, direct responses to analyst questions, and emphasis on strategic initiatives and AI investments, projecting a cautiously bullish sentiment overall.
